chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH CIRCUIT COURT MIRPURKHAS Crl. Bail Application No.S-51 of 2025 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE 1. For orders on M.A No.477/ 2025. 2. For orders on office objection. 3. For orders on MA No.478/ 2025. 4. For hearing of main case. 05.03.2025 Mr. Naveed Hussain Umrani, Advocate for the applicants a/w applicants. = 1 Urgency granted. 2 Deferred. 3 Exemption is granted subject to all just exceptions. 4 It is contended by the counsel for the applicants that due to strike in the District Bar Tando Allahyar it is not possible for the applicants to obtain interim pre-arrest bail from the Sessions Court Tando Allahyar. It is further contended that there is altercation between the parties, who are residing in neighborhood and the complainant lodged false FIR with malafide intention and ulterior motive under impersonated injuries due to pitty issue over agricultural land. It is further contended that the applicants have also filed complaint before SHO PS Chamber, but the SHO has not taken any action, therefore, they have filed application before Ex-Officio Justice of Peace U/S 22A&B Cr.P.C, which is pending before the learned Sessions Judge Tando Allahyar. Hence, without touching merits of the case, the applicants are granted protective bail in crime No.25/2025 U/Ss 506/2, 504, 341, 337-A(i), 337- F(i) PPC for a period of 10 days, enabling them to surrender before the trial Court on or before 15.03.2025 subject to furnishing a solvent surety 2 in the sum of Rs.20,000/-each and P.R bond in the like amount to the satisfaction of the learned Additional Registrar of this Court.

It is further contended that the applicants have also filed complaint before SHO PS Chamber, but the SHO has not taken any action, therefore, they have filed application before Ex-Officio Justice of Peace U/S 22A&B Cr.P.C, which is pending before the learned Sessions Judge Tando Allahyar. Hence, without touching merits of the case, the applicants are granted protective bail in crime No.25/2025 U/Ss 506/2, 504, 341, 337-A(i), 337- F(i) PPC for a period of 10 days, enabling them to surrender before the trial Court on or before 15.03.2025 subject to furnishing a solvent surety 2 in the sum of Rs.20,000/-each and P.R bond in the like amount to the satisfaction of the learned Additional Registrar of this Court. This order shall cease to have its effect on expiry of the aforesaid period or on the date on which the applicants surrender themselves before the Court concerned, whichever is earlier. It is made clear that in case applicants/accused fail to appear before the trial court, the surety so furnished by them shall be forfeited without notice. The protective bail application stands disposed of in above terms. JUDGE *Adnan Ashraf Nizamani*
